Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 172 [Udug-hul Compendium][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

en₂ a₂-sag₃ gig-ga su lu₂-u₁₉-lu-ke₄ mu-un-ŋal₂

(o 1) (Sumerian: Incantation). The grievous asakku-demon occurs in a man's body.

a-sak-ku mar-ṣu ina SU LU₂ it-tab-ši
o 2o 2

lu₂-u₁₉-lu pa₄-ḫal-la tug₂-gin₇ mu-un-dul

(o 2) On the restless man, he covered (him), like a garment:

ana a-me-lu mut-⸢tal-li⸣-ku GIM ṣu-ba-tu₂ ik-ta-tam
o 3o 3

šu-bi ŋiri₃-bi <nu>-mu-un-ši-in-ŋa₂-ŋa₂

(o 3) he cannot raise his hand or foot.

qa-a-su ⸢še⸣-ep-šu₂ ul i-na-ši1
o 4o 4

nun gal den-ki en ka-inim-ma-bi

(o 4) Ea, the great Prince, lord of the incantation,

ru-bu-u ra-bu-u de₂-a EN EN₂
o 55

d⸢en-ki⸣-e-ne-še₃ : ša₂ dMIN<(EN.KI-E.NE.ŠE₃)> u dMIN<(NIN.KI-E.NE.ŠE₃)> : dnin-ki-e-ne-še₃

(o 5) gave a male goat to the Enki and Ninki deities (Akkadian: concerning the Enki and Ninki deities)

o 6o 6

[maš₂]-saŋ gaba-ri-bi-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(o 6) as his (the man's) counterpart.

⸢u₂⸣-ri-ṣi ana me-eḫ-ri-šu₂ it-ta-din
o 7o 7

abgal gu₃ nam-mi-in-de₂

(o 7) He thus proclaimed to the sage:

ana ap-kal-lu is-si-[ma]
o 8o 8

maš₂ niŋ₂-saŋ-⸢il₂⸣-la ⸢min₃ lu₂⸣-[u₁₉-lu-ke₄]

(o 8) "The male goat is the subsitute for the human being!".

⸢u₂-ri-ṣa MIN?⸣<(NIG₂.SAG.IL₂-)>u₂ [ša₂] ⸢a⸣-me-⸢lu⸣-[ti]
o 9o 9

maš₂ zi-a-ni-še₃ ba-an-[šum₂]

(o 9) He gave the male goat for his life;

u₂-ri-ṣa ana na-piš-ti-šu₂ i-ta-din
o 10o 10

saŋ maš₂ saŋ lu₂-še₃ ba-an-⸢šum₂⸣

(o 10) he gave the goat's head for the man's head;

qaq-qa-du u₂-ri-⸢ṣa⸣ ana qaq-qa-du LU₂ i-ta-⸢din⸣
o 11o 11

gu₂ maš₂ gu₂ lu₂-⸢še₃⸣ ba-an-šum₂

(o 11) he gave the goat's neck for the man's neck;

ki-šad u₂-ri-ṣa ana ki-šad LU₂ i-ta-din
o 12o 12

gaba maš₂ gaba lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(o 12) he gave the goat's chest for the man's chest;

ir-ti u₂-ri-ṣa ana ir-ti LU₂ i-ta-din
o 13o 13

a₂ zid-da maš₂ a₂ zid-da lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(o 13) he gave the goat's right side for the man's right side;

i-mit-tu₂ u₂-⸢ri-ṣa⸣ ana ⸢i⸣-[mit]-tu₂ LU₂ i-ta-din
o 14o 14

a₂ gab₂-⸢bu⸣ maš₂ a₂ ⸢gab₂⸣-[bu] lu₂-⸢še₃⸣ ba-an-šum₂

(o 14) he gave the goat's left side for the man's left side;

šu-mel u₂-ri-ṣa ana šu-mel LU₂ i-ta-din
o 15o 15

mud₂ maš₂ mud₂ lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(o 15) he gave the goat's blood for the man's blood;

da-me u₂-ri-ṣa ana da-me LU₂ i-ta-din
Reverse
r 1r 1

lipiš maš₂ lipiš lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(r 1) he gave the goat's heart for the man's heart;

lib₃-bi u₂-ri-ṣi ana lib₃-bi LU₂ i-ta-din
r 2r 2

ti-ti maš₂ ti-ti lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(r 2) he gave the goat's ribs for the man's ribs;

ṣe-li u₂-ri-ṣi ana ṣe-li LU₂ i-ta-din
r 3r 3

gu₂-mur₇ maš₂ gu₂-mur₇ lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(r 3) he gave the goat's backbone for the man's backbone;

e-ṣe-en-ṣe-ri u₂-ri-ṣi ana <e>-ṣe-en-ṣe-ri LU₂ i-ta-din
r 4r 4

ur₂ maš₂ ur₂ lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(r 4) he gave the goat's upper thigh for the man's upper thigh;

pe-en u₂-ri-ṣi ana pe-en LU₂ i-ta-din
r 5r 5

ur₂-kun maš₂ ur₂-kun lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(r 5) he gave the goat's loin for the man's loin;

ra-pa-aš₂-ti u₂-ri-ṣi ana ra-pa-aš₂-ti LU₂ i-ta-din
r 6r 6

ŋeš₂-ge-en-ge-na maš₂ ŋeš₂-ge-en-ge-na lu₂-še₃ ba-an-šum₂

(r 6) he gave the goat's limbs for the man's limbs;

bi-na-at u₂-ri-ṣi ana bi-na-at LU₂ i-ta-din
r 77

tu₆-dug₄-ga inim den-ki-ga-ke₄

(r 7) Incantation, word of Enki.

r 88

⸢mu₇⸣-[mu₇] ⸢abzu nam-šub⸣ eriduki-ga nam-mu-un-da-an-bur₂-ra

(r 8) May the incantation of the Abzu, the incantation of Eridu, not be release!

r 99

ka-inim-ma [maš₂] ⸢gaba-ri⸣-ga-ke₄

(r 9) Formula for the goat's substitute.

(catchline)
r 1010

EGIR-šu₂ EN₂ ⸢NAM.TAR⸣ x x [...] ⸢GI₄?⸣

(r 10) After that, incantation: Namtar ... [...] ....

(colophon)
r 1111

GIŠ mdIM.DU.DU—ŠEŠ—MU

(r 11) Written document of Nabu-ah-iddin, for the viewing of Qurdi-Nergal.

r 1212

a-na ta-mar-ti mqur-di—dNERGALₓ(U.GUR)

1one line is missing here according to other versions.
